$comment: Machine-readable index of the skills in this repo. An AI browsing the repo can parse this to recommend the right skill. The authoritative trigger for each skill is the `description` in its SKILL.md frontmatter; this index summarizes it for selection.
install: Copy a skill folder into ~/.claude/skills/ (global) or <project>/.claude/skills/ (per-project). Claude Code auto-discovers it.

skills.name: deck-builder
skills.summary: Build or restructure polished, on-brand PowerPoint (.pptx) decks programmatically, with enforced layout/design rules, vector SVG icons, and a render-to-PNG self-check.
useWhen: creating a pitch deck or presentation
fixing slides that feel empty, repetitive, or inconsistent
skills.skipWhen: you only need to read or extract content from a .pptx (use the base `pptx` skill)

skills.name: review-contrib
skills.summary: Maintainer-side: audit, test and merge a collaborator's commit/branch/PR (often from a fork) safely, in an isolated worktree, never touching main.
useWhen: someone pushed a commit/branch/PR you need to adopt
you maintain a repo where main is production
skills.skipWhen: you are the one writing the contribution (use `contribuer`)

skills.name: contribuer
skills.summary: Contributor-side mirror of review-contrib: prepare a clean, adoptable PR to a repo you don't maintain — sync the fork, respect the project's immutable rules & conventions, pass its gate, open a small PR.
useWhen: you start coding a feature or fix on someone else's repo
you want your PR to be adopted fast
skills.skipWhen: you are the maintainer reviewing others' work (use `review-contrib`)
triggers: make a pull request to this repo

skills.name: autocli-password
skills.summary: Run a CLI command that needs a secret only the user knows via a native hidden-input dialog piped straight into the command. The secret stays in RAM — never in chat, history, or on disk. Optional ssh-agent-style RAM cache.
useWhen: a single command blocks on a credential (decrypt a vault, SSH passphrase, sudo, CLI login)
skills.skipWhen: you need many secrets at runtime or to replace .env (use `vault-secrets`)

skills.name: vault-secrets
skills.summary: Choose & configure a queryable secrets manager and use it in place of .env: a provider-agnostic façade (vault.sh) to store secrets without pasting them and inject them at runtime.
useWhen: choosing/wiring a password manager into scripts or CI
storing an API key or token safely
skills.skipWhen: you just need one interactive secret for one command (use `autocli-password`)
deps: one vault CLI (bw / op / pass / keepassxc-cli / doppler / infisical)
triggers: which password manager should I use

skills.name: handoff
skills.summary: Hand a Claude Code session between machines over git: a WIP commit on a safe branch + a HANDOFF.md brief + a ready-to-paste resume command. Optional autonomous remote agent and a config-driven target diagnostic. Softcoded per repo.
useWhen: continuing or delegating work on a VPS or another workstation
moving a session between your laptop and a server
skills.skipWhen: both ends already share a live workspace
triggers: pick up the work from the server
